description: A current sensor self-test shall be performed during each power-up initialisation. The test shall inject a known reference and verify within 2 % accuracy.
supplementary_info:
Function: Current Management
Section: 1 Overvoltage Protection > 1.1 Detection and Confirmation > 1.2 Warning and Derating > 1.3 Severe Fault and Recovery > 2 Current Management > 2.1 Overcurrent Detection > 2.2 Short Circuit Detection > 2.3 Current Sensor Fault
Context: Current sensor plausibility is verified against a model-predicted value based on pack voltage slope and operating mode. A sensor fault triggers degraded operation using voltage-based power estimation. Sensor self-test is performed during each power-up initialisation (see section 13):
- BMS_CurrSenseFault_Flag = 1: sensor deviation > 10 % from prediction
- BMS_DeratePower_Req = 1: request VCU to reduce power demand
- Fault persisting > 5 s triggers BMS_SafeState_Entry = 1

analysis.direction: triggering: BMS_CurrSenseFault_Flag = 1 when sensor deviation > 10 % from prediction; clearing: BMS_CurrSenseFault_Flag = 0 after fault persists < 5 s
missing_critical_info: response timing / debounce duration not specified

case_intents.description: Verify that the current sensor self-test is performed during each power-up initialization and checks for a deviation within 2% accuracy.
Test the boundary condition where the sensor deviation is exactly 10% to ensure proper flagging of BMS_CurrSenseFault_Flag.
Simulate a scenario where the current sensor fails and verify that BMS_DeratePower_Req = 1 is triggered, and power reduction is requested from VCU.
Test the state transition when a fault persists for less than 5 seconds to ensure BMS_CurrSenseFault_Flag clears.
Verify that the system enters safe state after a prolonged sensor fault (more than 5 seconds) by checking if BMS_SafeState_Entry = 1.

cases.title: Verify current sensor self-test during power-up initialization with 2% accuracy
cases.objective: To verify that the BMS performs a current sensor self-test during each power-up initialization and checks for a deviation within 2% accuracy.
cases.precondition: BMS is powered up, and the system is in an initial state awaiting self-test initiation.
cases.postcondition: The BMS system is in a normal operational state with the current sensor self-test results verified and no faults indicated.

steps.action: Power up the BMS system.
The BMS initiates a current sensor self-test during power-up initialization.
steps.expected: null [NEEDS REVIEW]

steps.action: BMS injects a known reference value into the current sensor.
steps.expected: null [NEEDS REVIEW]

steps.action: BMS verifies the injected reference against the predicted model value based on pack voltage slope and operating mode.
steps.expected: Ensure BMS_CurrSenseFault_Flag = 0, indicating no fault detected within 2% accuracy [NEEDS REVIEW]

description:
The BMS shall monitor the HV interlock loop continuously.
IF  HVIL circuit is interrupted
THEN  BMS_HVILFault_Flag := 1
  · All HV contactor closure prohibited
supplementary_info:
Function: Insulation & HVIL
Section: 1 Overvoltage Protection > 1.1 Detection and Confirmation > 1.2 Warning and Derating > 1.3 Severe Fault and Recovery > 2 Current Management > 2.1 Overcurrent Detection > 2.2 Short Circuit Detection > 2.3 Current Sensor Fault > 3 Thermal Management > 3.1 Over-Temperature Protection > 3.2 Under-Temperature Protection > 3.3 Temperature Imbalance > 4 SOC/SOH Estimation > 4.1 Low SOC Monitoring > 4.2 SOH Degradation Monitoring > 5 Cell Balancing > 5.1 Balancing Detection and Activation > 6 Insulation and HVIL > 6.1 Insulation Resistance Monitoring > 6.2 HV Interlock Monitoring
Context: The HV interlock loop (HVIL) provides a hardware-based detection path for connector disconnection or harness damage. When the circuit is interrupted, the system must immediately isolate the HV system to prevent exposure to live terminals:
- BMS_HVILFault_Flag = 1: HVIL circuit interrupted
- Response: all contactors open within 100 ms, BMS_SystemState = SafeState

analysis.direction:
triggering: BMS_HVILFault_Flag is set to 1 when the HV interlock loop is interrupted.
clearing: All contactors open and BMS_SystemState changes to SafeState within 100 ms after BMS_HVILFault_Flag is set.
missing_critical_info: none

case_intents.description: Test the normal behavior of setting BMS_HVILFault_Flag when the HV interlock loop is interrupted and ensure all contactors open within 100 ms, with BMS_SystemState transitioning to SafeState.
Test the boundary condition where the HV interlock loop is just about to be interrupted but not yet. This could involve a gradual degradation or threshold approach to the fault state.

Simulate an extreme case where the HV interlock loop fails completely and verify that BMS_HVILFault_Flag is set immediately, all contactors open within 10 ms (earlier than specified), and BMS_SystemState transitions to SafeState.
Verify the state transition from normal operation to the SafeState after BMS_HVILFault_Flag is triggered due to an interruption in the HV interlock loop.
Test that the system provides observable feedback through BMS_HVILFault_Flag and other relevant signals when the HV interlock loop is interrupted, ensuring proper fault indication.

cases.title: BMS_HVILFault_Flag set and contactors open when HV interlock loop is interrupted
cases.objective: To verify the BMS correctly sets BMS_HVILFault_Flag to 1 and all contactors are opened within 100 ms, transitioning the system state to SafeState upon interruption of the HV interlock loop.
cases.precondition: BMS in normal operation with HV contactors closed and BMS_HVILFault_Flag = 0
cases.postcondition: BMS_HVILFault_Flag = 1, all contactors are open, and system state is in SafeState.

steps.action: Simulate an interruption in the HV interlock loop (HVIL) circuit by opening the HVIL switch or removing power from the HVIL sensor.
Wait for 100 ms to observe the BMS response.
steps.expected: BMS_HVILFault_Flag := 1; All contactors open; BMS_SystemState = SafeState

description:
IF  any degradation indicator exceeds its threshold
THEN
  · BMS_CellDegrade_Warn := 1
  · Update SOH prediction model
supplementary_info:
Function: Degradation Monitoring
Section: 1 Overvoltage Protection > 1.1 Detection and Confirmation > 1.2 Warning and Derating > 1.3 Severe Fault and Recovery > 2 Current Management > 2.1 Overcurrent Detection > 2.2 Short Circuit Detection > 2.3 Current Sensor Fault > 3 Thermal Management > 3.1 Over-Temperature Protection > 3.2 Under-Temperature Protection > 3.3 Temperature Imbalance > 4 SOC/SOH Estimation > 4.1 Low SOC Monitoring > 4.2 SOH Degradation Monitoring > 5 Cell Balancing > 5.1 Balancing Detection and Activation > 6 Insulation and HVIL > 6.1 Insulation Resistance Monitoring > 6.2 HV Interlock Monitoring > 6.3 HV Leakage Detection > 7 Communication and Diagnostics > 7.1 CAN Bus Fault > 7.2 VCU Heartbeat Monitoring > 8 Undervoltage Protection > 8.1 Detection and Response > 9 Charge Management > 9.1 Charge Initiation > 9.2 Charge Termination > 10 Pack Voltage Protection > 10.1 Pack Voltage Monitoring > 11 Degradation Monitoring > 11.1 Cell Degradation Detection

analysis.direction: The requirement describes a detection and confirmation behavior, where the BMS sets the warning flag when a degradation indicator exceeds its threshold.
missing_critical_info: response timing / debounce duration not specified

case_intents.description: Test the normal behavior of setting BMS_CellDegrade_Warn to 1 when any degradation indicator exceeds its threshold.
Test the boundary condition where a degradation indicator is exactly at or slightly above its threshold value.
Test the scenario where multiple degradation indicators exceed their thresholds simultaneously to ensure proper handling and model update.
Test the state transition from normal operation to the degraded state, including the update of SOH prediction model.
Verify that the BMS_CellDegrade_Warn signal is correctly updated and observable under various threshold conditions.

description:
IF  total pack voltage < minimum operating threshold
THEN
  · BMS_PackUV_Flag := 1
  · Send DTC P0A01 to VCU
  · Limit discharge power proportionally to undervoltage severity
supplementary_info:
Function: Pack Voltage Protection
Section: 1 Overvoltage Protection > 1.1 Detection and Confirmation > 1.2 Warning and Derating > 1.3 Severe Fault and Recovery > 2 Current Management > 2.1 Overcurrent Detection > 2.2 Short Circuit Detection > 2.3 Current Sensor Fault > 3 Thermal Management > 3.1 Over-Temperature Protection > 3.2 Under-Temperature Protection > 3.3 Temperature Imbalance > 4 SOC/SOH Estimation > 4.1 Low SOC Monitoring > 4.2 SOH Degradation Monitoring > 5 Cell Balancing > 5.1 Balancing Detection and Activation > 6 Insulation and HVIL > 6.1 Insulation Resistance Monitoring > 6.2 HV Interlock Monitoring > 6.3 HV Leakage Detection > 7 Communication and Diagnostics > 7.1 CAN Bus Fault > 7.2 VCU Heartbeat Monitoring > 8 Undervoltage Protection > 8.1 Detection and Response > 9 Charge Management > 9.1 Charge Initiation > 9.2 Charge Termination > 10 Pack Voltage Protection > 10.1 Pack Voltage Monitoring
Context: Pack voltage monitoring provides a system-level check that complements individual cell monitoring (section 8). A pack undervoltage or sensor plausibility fault is reported to the VCU with DTC. The pack voltage measurement is cross-checked against the sum of cell voltages to detect sensor faults:
- BMS_PackUV_Flag = 1: pack voltage < minimum operating threshold, DTC P0A01 sent
- BMS_PackVoltSensorFault_Flag = 1: cell-sum to pack measurement deviation > 5 V

thresholds: minimum operating threshold (not a specific signal or parameter)
analysis.direction: triggering: BMS_PackUV_Flag := 1 when total pack voltage < minimum operating threshold; sending DTC P0A01 to VCU
missing_critical_info: response timing / debounce duration not specified

description:
IF  charge current <= 3 A AND max cell voltage >= 4.18 V
THEN
  · BMS_ChargeComplete_Flag := 1
  · Terminate charge session
supplementary_info:
Function: Charge Management
Section: 1 Overvoltage Protection > 1.1 Detection and Confirmation > 1.2 Warning and Derating > 1.3 Severe Fault and Recovery > 2 Current Management > 2.1 Overcurrent Detection > 2.2 Short Circuit Detection > 2.3 Current Sensor Fault > 3 Thermal Management > 3.1 Over-Temperature Protection > 3.2 Under-Temperature Protection > 3.3 Temperature Imbalance > 4 SOC/SOH Estimation > 4.1 Low SOC Monitoring > 4.2 SOH Degradation Monitoring > 5 Cell Balancing > 5.1 Balancing Detection and Activation > 6 Insulation and HVIL > 6.1 Insulation Resistance Monitoring > 6.2 HV Interlock Monitoring > 6.3 HV Leakage Detection > 7 Communication and Diagnostics > 7.1 CAN Bus Fault > 7.2 VCU Heartbeat Monitoring > 8 Undervoltage Protection > 8.1 Detection and Response > 9 Charge Management > 9.1 Charge Initiation > 9.2 Charge Termination
Context: Charge termination is triggered by normal completion criteria (current taper indicating full charge), external requests, or fault conditions from other protection functions (e.g., overvoltage from section 1). A charge event record is stored for diagnostic and lifecycle tracking:
- BMS_ChargeComplete_Flag = 1: charge current <= 3 A AND max cell >= 4.18 V
- BMS_ChargeTerm_Req = 1: termination requested (from charge complete or section 1)
- Recorded data: final SOC, energy transferred, session duration

missing_critical_info: response timing / debounce duration not specified

Test a fault condition where the charge current exceeds 3 A, ensuring BMS_ChargeComplete_Flag remains unchanged and no charge termination occurs.

Test the state transition from charging to complete by setting BMS_ChargeTerm_Req = 1 while charge current is 2.99 A and max cell voltage is 4.17 V, ensuring BMS_ChargeComplete_Flag is set to 1 and charge session termination occurs.

description:
Upon BMS_CellOV_Flag = 1, the BMS shall:
  · Broadcast BMS_CellOV_Warn = 1 on CAN ID 0x180 at 100 ms periodicity
  · Reduce charge current target to 50 % of nominal
supplementary_info:
Function: Overvoltage Protection
Section: 1 Overvoltage Protection > 1.1 Detection and Confirmation > 1.2 Warning and Derating
Context: When a confirmed overvoltage fault is present, the BMS initiates a controlled derating phase before resorting to contactor-level actions. Warning signals are broadcast on CAN for VCU coordination:
- BMS_CellOV_Warn = 1: overvoltage confirmed (BMS_CellOV_Flag = 1)
  Transmission: CAN ID 0x180, periodicity 100 ms
- BMS_ChargeTerm_Req = 1: charge termination requested
- Charge current target reduced to 50 % of nominal during derating

analysis.direction: triggering
missing_critical_info: response timing / debounce duration not specified

case_intents.description: Test the normal behavior where BMS_CellOV_Flag transitions from 0 to 1, causing BMS_CellOV_Warn to be broadcast on CAN at 100 ms periodicity and charge current target to reduce to 50% of nominal.
Verify that the system does not broadcast BMS_CellOV_Warn if BMS_CellOV_Flag is set but the periodicity condition is not met (e.g., less than 100 ms between broadcasts).
Ensure that setting BMS_CellOV_Flag to 0 after it has been triggered does not immediately reset BMS_ChargeTerm_Req or restore charge current target.
Test the state transition from normal operation to derating mode and back, ensuring all signals change as expected when BMS_CellOV_Flag transitions between 0 and 1.

cases.postcondition: BMS_CellOV_Flag = 1, BMS_CellOV_Warn = 1, Charge_current_target = 50% of nominal, BMS_ChargeTerm_Req = 0

description:
IF  BMS_ChargeTerm_Req = 1 AND any cell voltage >= r_CellOV_L3_Threshold
THEN
  · BMS_CellOV_L3_Flag := 1
supplementary_info:
Function: Overvoltage Protection
Section: 1 Overvoltage Protection > 1.1 Detection and Confirmation > 1.2 Warning and Derating > 1.3 Severe Fault and Recovery
Context: If the overvoltage condition escalates beyond Level 2 despite derating, immediate contactor opening is required to protect the battery. Contactors are opened in sequence and the system transitions to a safe state:
- BMS_ChargeContactor_State: 0 = Open, 1 = Closed
- BMS_DischargeContactor_State: 0 = Open, 1 = Closed
- BMS_SystemState transitions to SafeState; refer to section 13 for state definitions
- Recovery requires all cell voltages below 4.00 V for >= 5 s

missing_critical_info: response timing / debounce duration not specified

description:
IF  max cell voltage delta >= 50 mV AND SOC > 30 %
THEN
  · Activate passive cell balancing
  · Select cells with highest voltage for discharge
supplementary_info:
Function: Cell Balancing
Section: 1 Overvoltage Protection > 1.1 Detection and Confirmation > 1.2 Warning and Derating > 1.3 Severe Fault and Recovery > 2 Current Management > 2.1 Overcurrent Detection > 2.2 Short Circuit Detection > 2.3 Current Sensor Fault > 3 Thermal Management > 3.1 Over-Temperature Protection > 3.2 Under-Temperature Protection > 3.3 Temperature Imbalance > 4 SOC/SOH Estimation > 4.1 Low SOC Monitoring > 4.2 SOH Degradation Monitoring > 5 Cell Balancing > 5.1 Balancing Detection and Activation
Context: Cell balancing compensates for voltage differences between cells to maximise usable capacity and prevent individual cells from exceeding safe voltage limits. Balancing is only active when the pack SOC is sufficiently high and pack current is low. Each balancing event is logged with start and end timestamps, discharged cells, and delta before and after:
- Activation: max delta >= 50 mV AND SOC > 30 %
- Deactivation: delta < 20 mV or balancing timeout

analysis.direction:
triggering: Activation of passive cell balancing when max delta >= 50 mV AND SOC > 30 %
clearing: Deactivation of cell balancing when delta < 20 mV or balancing timeout
missing_critical_info: response timing / debounce duration not specified

case_intents.description: Test the normal behavior where the BMS activates passive cell balancing when the maximum cell voltage difference is greater than 50 mV and the SOC is above 30%.
Test the boundary condition where the maximum cell voltage difference is exactly 50 mV and the SOC is exactly 30% to ensure the system does not activate balancing in this case.
Test the fault condition where the maximum cell voltage difference is less than 20 mV or a balancing timeout occurs, ensuring that cell balancing deactivates correctly.
Test the state transition from no balancing to active balancing and back to inactive balancing based on changes in max delta and SOC values.
Verify that logging during cell balancing events includes correct start and end timestamps, discharged cells, and voltage deltas before and after.

description:
Upon VCU_HVConnect_Req = 1 with all preconditions satisfied:
  · Close negative main contactor
  · Enable precharge circuit
  · BMS_SystemState := PrechargeActive
supplementary_info:
Function: BMS State Machine
Section: 1 Overvoltage Protection > 1.1 Detection and Confirmation > 1.2 Warning and Derating > 1.3 Severe Fault and Recovery > 2 Current Management > 2.1 Overcurrent Detection > 2.2 Short Circuit Detection > 2.3 Current Sensor Fault > 3 Thermal Management > 3.1 Over-Temperature Protection > 3.2 Under-Temperature Protection > 3.3 Temperature Imbalance > 4 SOC/SOH Estimation > 4.1 Low SOC Monitoring > 4.2 SOH Degradation Monitoring > 5 Cell Balancing > 5.1 Balancing Detection and Activation > 6 Insulation and HVIL > 6.1 Insulation Resistance Monitoring > 6.2 HV Interlock Monitoring > 6.3 HV Leakage Detection > 7 Communication and Diagnostics > 7.1 CAN Bus Fault > 7.2 VCU Heartbeat Monitoring > 8 Undervoltage Protection > 8.1 Detection and Response > 9 Charge Management > 9.1 Charge Initiation > 9.2 Charge Termination > 10 Pack Voltage Protection > 10.1 Pack Voltage Monitoring > 11 Degradation Monitoring > 11.1 Cell Degradation Detection > 12 Fault Management > 12.1 Unrecoverable Fault Handling > 13 BMS State Machine > 13.1 Power-Up and Initialization > 13.2 HV Activation
Context: HV activation follows a precharge sequence to safely energise the DC link before closing the positive contactor, preventing inrush current damage. The sequence is aborted if the bus voltage does not rise within the expected time. Up to 2 retries are permitted before locking out HV activation until power cycle:
- Preconditions: VCU_HVConnect_Req = 1, no active faults, isolation OK, contactors Open, pack voltage in range
- Success criteria: DC bus >= 90 % of pack voltage within precharge timeout
- Failure: BMS_SystemState = HV_Failure, BMS_PrechargeFault_Flag = 1

analysis.direction: triggering: Close negative main contactor, enable precharge circuit; clearing: BMS_SystemState = HV_Failure, BMS_PrechargeFault_Flag = 1
missing_critical_info: response timing / debounce duration not specified

case_intents.description: Test the normal behavior of the system when VCU_HVConnect_Req is asserted and all preconditions are satisfied, ensuring that the negative main contactor closes, the precharge circuit enables, and BMS_SystemState transitions to PrechargeActive.
Test the boundary condition where the DC bus voltage rises just below 90% of the pack voltage within the precharge timeout period.

Test the fault scenario where VCU_HVConnect_Req is asserted but the bus voltage does not rise to at least 90% of the pack voltage within the precharge timeout, resulting in BMS_SystemState = HV_Failure and BMS_PrechargeFault_Flag = 1.
Test the state transition from PrechargeActive back to a safe state if VCU_HVConnect_Req is de-asserted or other preconditions are not met during the precharge sequence.

cases.title: Test the normal behavior of BMS when VCU_HVConnect_Req is asserted and all preconditions are satisfied
cases.objective: To verify that upon VCU_HVConnect_Req = 1 with all preconditions satisfied, the negative main contactor closes, the precharge circuit enables, and BMS_SystemState transitions to PrechargeActive.

case_intents.description: Test the normal behavior of setting BMS_LowSOC_Flag and limiting discharge power to 25% of MaxDischargePower when SOC is less than or equal to 10%.

Validate that the state change in BMS_SOHEstFault_Flag and the SOH re-calibration request can be observed and logged correctly.

analysis.direction: The requirement describes a triggering behavior. description:
While BMS_ChargeTerm_Req = 1:
  · Charge contactor closure shall be inhibited
  · BMS_ChargeReady_Flag := 0
  · Charge termination status shall be broadcast on CAN
supplementary_info:
Function: Overvoltage Protection
Section: 1 Overvoltage Protection > 1.1 Detection and Confirmation > 1.2 Warning and Derating

analysis.direction: triggering: BMS_ChargeTerm_Req = 1 shall trigger the inhibition of charge contactor closure, setting BMS_ChargeReady_Flag to 0, and broadcasting charge termination status on CAN.
missing_critical_info: response timing / debounce duration not specified

case_intents.description: Test that when BMS_ChargeTerm_Req = 1, the system correctly inhibits charge contactor closure, sets BMS_ChargeReady_Flag to 0, and broadcasts charge termination status on CAN.
Test boundary conditions for BMS_ChargeTerm_Req (e.g., when it transitions from 0 to 1).
Test the system's response to a fault condition that would cause BMS_ChargeTerm_Req to be set to 1.
Test state transitions related to charge termination status broadcasting on CAN.
Verify observability of the system's response by monitoring BMS_ChargeReady_Flag and CAN messages during normal operation.

cases.postcondition: BMS_ChargeTerm_Req = 1; BMS_ChargeReady_Flag = 0; Charge contactor remains open; charge termination status has been broadcast on CAN.

description:
IF  unrecoverable internal fault detected
THEN
  · Transition to SafeState
  · Open all contactors
  · BMS_Fault_Latch := 1
  · Broadcast fault status on CAN
supplementary_info:
Function: Fault Management
Section: 1 Overvoltage Protection > 1.1 Detection and Confirmation > 1.2 Warning and Derating > 1.3 Severe Fault and Recovery > 2 Current Management > 2.1 Overcurrent Detection > 2.2 Short Circuit Detection > 2.3 Current Sensor Fault > 3 Thermal Management > 3.1 Over-Temperature Protection > 3.2 Under-Temperature Protection > 3.3 Temperature Imbalance > 4 SOC/SOH Estimation > 4.1 Low SOC Monitoring > 4.2 SOH Degradation Monitoring > 5 Cell Balancing > 5.1 Balancing Detection and Activation > 6 Insulation and HVIL > 6.1 Insulation Resistance Monitoring > 6.2 HV Interlock Monitoring > 6.3 HV Leakage Detection > 7 Communication and Diagnostics > 7.1 CAN Bus Fault > 7.2 VCU Heartbeat Monitoring > 8 Undervoltage Protection > 8.1 Detection and Response > 9 Charge Management > 9.1 Charge Initiation > 9.2 Charge Termination > 10 Pack Voltage Protection > 10.1 Pack Voltage Monitoring > 11 Degradation Monitoring > 11.1 Cell Degradation Detection > 12 Fault Management > 12.1 Unrecoverable Fault Handling
Context: Unrecoverable internal faults represent failures from which the BMS cannot safely continue normal operation. These include hardware integrity failures and critical sensor path loss. Recovery from this state requires a power cycle, and the fault code is retained for diagnostic readout:
- Memory error (ECC uncorrectable), critical sensor failure, watchdog timeout
- Response: SafeState, all contactors open, BMS_Fault_Latch = 1, power cycle required

analysis.direction: triggering: BMS_Fault_Latch := 1 when unrecoverable internal fault detected.
missing_critical_info: response timing / debounce duration not specified

case_intents.description: Test the normal behavior of transitioning to SafeState and opening all contactors upon detection of an unrecoverable internal fault.
Verify that BMS_Fault_Latch is set to 1 when an unrecoverable internal fault is detected.
Ensure the system broadcasts a fault status on CAN upon detection of an unrecoverable internal fault.
Test the state transition from normal operation to SafeState when an unrecoverable internal fault is detected.
Confirm that the BMS_Fault_Latch signal accurately reflects the presence of an unrecoverable internal fault.

description:
IF  BMS_CellOV_Detect = 1 for a continuous duration >= t_CellOV_Debounce
THEN
  · BMS_CellOV_Flag := 1
  · Store the maximum cell voltage and affected cell ID in the fault record
supplementary_info:
Function: Overvoltage Protection
Section: 1 Overvoltage Protection > 1.1 Detection and Confirmation

analysis.direction: triggering
missing_critical_info: none

case_intents.description: Test the normal behavior where BMS_CellOV_Detect remains high for a duration equal to or greater than t_CellOV_Debounce, resulting in BMS_CellOV_Flag being set to 1 and fault record updates.
Test the boundary condition where BMS_CellOV_Detect is high for exactly t_CellOV_Debounce duration, ensuring BMS_CellOV_Flag is set to 1.
Test a scenario where BMS_CellOV_Detect transitions from 0 to 1 and back to 0 within the debounce period, ensuring BMS_CellOV_Flag does not get set.
Test the state transition of BMS_CellOV_Detect going high for more than t_CellOV_Debounce duration followed by a reset, verifying that BMS_CellOV_Flag is cleared when BMS_CellOV_Detect returns to 0.
Verify that during an overvoltage event lasting at least t_CellOV_Debounce, the maximum cell voltage and affected cell ID are correctly stored in the fault record.

cases.objective: To verify the normal behavior where BMS_CellOV_Detect remains high for a continuous duration equal to or greater than t_CellOV_Debounce, resulting in BMS_CellOV_Flag being set to 1 and fault record updates.
cases.precondition: BMS is initialized and no overvoltage faults have been recorded. BMS_CellOV_Detect is currently low (0).
cases.postcondition: BMS_CellOV_Flag is set to 1, indicating an overvoltage condition was detected for a duration equal to or greater than t_CellOV_Debounce. The fault record contains the maximum cell voltage and the affected cell ID.

steps.action: Apply an overvoltage condition that causes BMS_CellOV_Detect to transition to high (1) for a duration of t_CellOV_Debounce or more.
Wait for the duration of t_CellOV_Debounce after BMS_CellOV_Detect transitions to 1.
steps.expected: BMS_CellOV_Flag := 1; Store the maximum cell voltage and affected cell ID in the fault record.

description:

  · Charger disconnected

description:
IF  calculated SOC <= 20 %
THEN
  · BMS_LowSOC_Warn := 1
  · Command instrument cluster low battery lamp ON
supplementary_info:
Function: SOC/SOH Estimation
Section: 1 Overvoltage Protection > 1.1 Detection and Confirmation > 1.2 Warning and Derating > 1.3 Severe Fault and Recovery > 2 Current Management > 2.1 Overcurrent Detection > 2.2 Short Circuit Detection > 2.3 Current Sensor Fault > 3 Thermal Management > 3.1 Over-Temperature Protection > 3.2 Under-Temperature Protection > 3.3 Temperature Imbalance > 4 SOC/SOH Estimation > 4.1 Low SOC Monitoring
Context: SOC monitoring provides the driver with range information and protects the battery from deep discharge by enforcing progressive power limits. The two-level warning scheme distinguishes between driver notification and actionable limitation:
- BMS_LowSOC_Warn = 1: SOC <= 20 % (advisory, instrument cluster lamp ON)
- BMS_LowSOC_Flag = 1: SOC <= 10 % (discharge limited to 25 % of MaxDischargePower)

analysis.direction: triggering: BMS_LowSOC_Warn := 1 when calculated SOC <= 20 %
missing_critical_info: response timing / debounce duration not specified

case_intents.description: Test the normal behavior of setting BMS_LowSOC_Warn to 1 when the calculated SOC is less than or equal to 20%.
Test the boundary condition where the calculated SOC is exactly 20% and verify that BMS_LowSOC_Warn is set to 1.
Verify the state transition from BMS_LowSOC_Warn = 0 to BMS_LowSOC_Warn = 1 as the calculated SOC decreases through 20%.
Ensure that the instrument cluster low battery lamp is turned ON when BMS_LowSOC_Warn is set to 1.

cases.title: Verify setting of BMS_LowSOC_Warn to 1 when calculated SOC is <= 20%
cases.objective: To verify that the BMS sets BMS_LowSOC_Warn to 1 and commands the instrument cluster low battery lamp ON when the calculated SOC is less than or equal to 20%.
cases.precondition: BMS is initialized, and the calculated SOC is set to a value <= 20 %.
cases.postcondition: BMS_LowSOC_Warn is set to 1, and the instrument cluster low battery lamp is commanded ON. The system state reflects a warning for low SOC.

steps.action: Set the calculated SOC to a value <= 20 %.
Observe the BMS.

<testcase>
<title>Verify setting of BMS_LowSOC_Warn to 1 when calculated SOC is <= 20%</title>
<objective>To verify that the BMS sets BMS_LowSOC_Warn to 1 and commands the instrument cluster low battery lamp ON when the calculated SOC is less than or equal to 20%.</objective>
<precondition>BMS is initialized, and the calculated SOC is set to a value <= 20 %.</precondition>
<steps>
<step order="1">
<action>Set the calculated SOC to a value <= 20 %.</action>
<expected>null</expected>
</step>
<step order="2">
<action>Observe the BMS.</action>
<expected>BMS_LowSOC_Warn := 1; Command instrument cluster low battery lamp ON [NEEDS REVIEW]</expected>
</step>
</steps>
<postcondition>BMS_LowSOC_Warn is set to 1, and the instrument cluster low battery lamp is commanded ON. The system state reflects a warning for low SOC.</postcondition>
</testcase>
